name: Conditioned medium meta-analysis supported skin antiaging endpoints
summary: A meta-analysis of RCTs found that topical stem-cell conditioned medium enhanced facial resurfacing effects on wrinkle, pigmentation, pore, and overall skin outcomes.
statement: A systematic review and meta-analysis included five randomized controlled trials and reported significant improvements for wrinkles, pigmentation, pores, and overall skin condition when topical stem-cell conditioned medium was added to resurfacing modalities.
population_or_model: Human facial skin-aging RCTs of conditioned medium plus nonsurgical resurfacing
time_horizon: Short dermatologic treatment and follow-up windows across included RCTs
quantitative_note: The reported meta-analysis p values were p = 0.0006 for wrinkles, p = 0.004 for pigmentation, p = 0.01 for pores, and p < 0.0001 for overall skin condition.
caveats: The evidence aggregates dermatologic appearance and instrumented skin endpoints rather than systemic aging outcomes.
Conditioned media are complex secretomes and may not isolate extracellular vesicle-specific mechanisms.
The source rated the evidence as Level III and included heterogeneous resurfacing modalities.
